document.critique: Rechecked all five candidates for three-per-machine counts, costs, and every interaction. Candidates 1–4 fail: 1 has incorrect counts and violates the J6→J7 implication; 2 fails the exact-one-Y rule; 3 has two of J12/J11/J5 on X; 4 has none of J2/J10/J7 on Y. Candidate 0 satisfies all rules and sums to 140, so the preceding proposal should be retained.

Rechecked capacities, all separation/sameness and implication rules, plus both exactly-one constraints. Candidate 3 survives and its costs sum to 166. Candidates 0 and 1 are feasible but cost more; candidate 2 fails capacity and J2/J11 separation; candidate 4 fails the J3=Z implication because J9=X. The preceding proposal is therefore retained as the unique optimum.

Recomputed every job cost and all capacities, domains, implications, same/different, and exact-one constraints. The proposed assignment is feasible and sums to 114. Candidate 0 is the same assignment but its 93 is arithmetically false; candidate 4 sums to 116, not 114. Thus the preceding proposal should be retained.

Rechecked all five candidates for three-job machine counts, every interaction, and exact cost. Candidate 2 has counts W/X/Y/Z = 3 each, satisfies all rules, and sums to 174. Candidate 0 violates the J10→J1 rule; Candidate 1 violates J1/J9 separation; Candidate 3 has incorrect capacities; Candidate 4 has zero jobs from J7,J5,J6 on X. The preceding proposal survives and is retained.

I directly substituted the proposed assignment into all 17 statements. Most entries match their speakers, but F does not: F's statement evaluates true while F is labeled liar. Thus the preceding proposal cannot be retained; none of the five candidates survives the same full consistency check, so a corrected exact assignment is required.

I evaluated every statement under each candidate’s proposed types. Candidates 0, 2, and 3 each fail at least one speaker equation. Candidate 1 fails only because O is labeled liar while O’s statement evaluates true. Changing O to truthful makes all 17 speaker/type equations consistent, so this corrected assignment replaces the preceding proposal.

I tested each candidate against its speakers’ full Boolean statements. Candidates 0–2 fail at A or C; candidates 3–4 fail at E or D. The preceding proposal also fails A: its implication’s antecedent is true but its consequent is false. Re-solving the linked conditions yields the returned assignment, where every statement matches its speaker’s type.

I re-evaluated all 17 statements under every candidate. Candidate 1 satisfies every type/statement equivalence except K: its statement evaluates true because OR(L,G)=true and XOR(L,F)=true, contradicting K as a liar. Candidate 3 fails N, while the others also fail at least one constraint. Therefore no listed candidate is an exact solution; the preceding proposal should not be retained.

I rechecked all admissible moduli and coefficient choices against every supplied recurrence equation, then verified the generated n=18,19,20 terms. Candidate 0 is the unique full-periodic match. Candidate 1 merely repeats the prefix, Candidate 2 fails immediately, and Candidate 3 may match an isolated relation but fails elsewhere. Thus the preceding proposal should be retained.

I rechecked the n=4, 8, 12, and 16 congruences, including periodic coefficient alignment, modular differences, and the n−3 terms. The tested alternatives fail these constraints; the surviving continuation is Candidate 3, so the preceding proposal should be retained.

I rechecked the n=4,8,12,16 difference equations. Their combined constraints force t=-2, M=97, and (p0,q0)=(2,-1) or (-2,1), but the independent equation 29p0−33q0+64t ≡ −40 fails for both. Thus the stated data are inconsistent; no candidate is verifiable. I retain the preceding proposal only as the strongest intended continuation.

I rechecked the recurrence constraints across the repeated residue classes at n=4, 8, 12, and 16, including the shared t term, 4-step r increment, and all three modulus options. Candidate 1 (0,0,0) does not satisfy the surviving constrained system; the unique continuation remains three residues of -1. Thus the preceding proposal is retained.
